title: RelationEdge
description: Standalone relation edge definition for the knowledge graph. Mirrors RelationRef but as a first-class entity with audit fields.

source_id.description: Source node ID (must exist in index.json)

target_id.description: Target node ID (must exist in index.json)

relation_type.description: Semantic relation type between source and target nodes

label.description: Optional human-readable description of this specific edge

direction.description: Whether the relation is one-way (source→target) or two-way

context.description: Free-text context explaining why this relation exists

RelationTypeGuide.description: Semantic guide for each relation_type value

const.parent: This node is a child of target. Use for hierarchical containment (task belongs to project).
const.child: This node is a parent of target. Mirror of parent — typically auto-derived.
const.implements: This node implements/executes target (e.g. code implements PRD requirement).
const.references: This node cites or reads from target, but is not derived from it.
const.extends: This node builds on or adds to target (e.g. v2 extends v1 design).
const.contradicts: This node's conclusion contradicts target. Useful for surfacing conflicts.
const.triggers: This node caused or initiated target (e.g. retro triggered a new task).
const.validates: This node proves, tests, or validates target (e.g. test validates implementation).
const.supersedes: This node replaces target, which is now deprecated.
const.related: Generic weak relation when none of the above precisely applies.
const.depends_on: This node cannot function without target being resolved/available.
const.blocks: This node is blocking progress on target.
